description: Surface contamination observed on terminal lug, light oxidation present. Cleaned and re-tested within spec.
Belt tracking drift of 3mm to operator side noted over 2-hour run. No product damage.
Leakage current 3.8 mA vs spec less than 2.0 mA on unit serial 7741. Quarantined for rework.
Dielectric absorption ratio fell to 0.42 after 1-min charge; expected greater than 0.6. Indicates moisture ingress in stack.
Bearing temperature elevated to 62C, threshold 70C. Within tolerance but trending upward over 48h.
Spot weld nugget diameter measured 3.1mm vs spec 4.0mm minimum on three consecutive samples. Electrode dressing performed.
Capacitance value 218 uF vs nominal 250 uF, drift exceeds 10 percent tolerance band. Unit pulled from line.
Minor scuff on outer kraft layer, no penetration. Cosmetic only, passed dielectric strength test.
Ultimate vacuum reached only 8e-3 mbar after 45min, spec is 5e-3 mbar within 30min. Pump oil scheduled for replacement.
Arc-over event during 800V hipot test; bank tripped breaker on test cell 2. Unit isolated, root cause investigation opened.
Sensor reading drift of 0.15 bar versus calibrated reference. Within service interval, flagged for next PM.
Oil dielectric breakdown voltage measured 38 kV, minimum spec 50 kV. Filtration cycle initiated.
Flow rate reading 14.2 L/min, nominal 15.0 L/min. Slight restriction suspected at heat exchanger inlet.
Winding resistance measured 0.84 ohm versus spec 0.75 ohm plus or minus 5 percent. Possible inter-turn short under investigation.
ESR elevated to 18 milliohm on production lot 2026-0316-A, baseline is 12 milliohm. Three of twenty samples affected.
Electrode tip showed mushrooming after 800 cycles, expected 1200 cycles. Replaced during planned changeover.
Visible micro-fracture along dielectric edge of two units, visible under 10x magnification. Suspect handling damage upstream.
Intermittent humming, audible from 3m distance during no-load run. Vibration analysis pending.
Belt splice showed delamination on 12mm section of edge. Temporary repair applied, replacement scheduled.
Audible click during reversal cycle on second-stage piston. No performance impact measured.
Slight oil weep at gasket interface on north-side flange. Bolts re-torqued to 45 Nm.
Stack height measured 142.3mm, spec 142.0 plus or minus 0.5mm. Within tolerance but at upper edge.
Output signal exhibits 2 percent peak-to-peak noise on 4-20mA loop. Shielding integrity check ordered.
Coil impregnation showed minor void at 11 o'clock position, less than 2mm diameter. Within acceptance criteria.
Self-discharge time constant of 8 minutes versus expected 15 minutes minimum. Two units from same lot affected.
Cracked weld nugget detected on cross-section sample, full-thickness fracture. Lot held pending 100 percent re-inspection.
pH of coolant trended down to 7.9, target range 8.5 to 9.5. Inhibitor dosing increased.
Catastrophic case rupture during life test at 1.2x rated voltage; electrolyte vented. Test chamber sealed, FMEA review triggered.
Phase current imbalance of 8 percent between U and W phases at full load. Stator winding inspection scheduled.
Oil sight glass shows slight darkening, particle count not yet exceeded. Sample sent to lab.
Label print quality degraded on three units, barcode unreadable on first scan. Reprinted and reapplied.
Partial discharge measured 25 pC at 1.5x Un, limit 10 pC. Suggests internal void in pressboard layer.
Roller bearing showing slight axial play, estimated 0.3mm. Not yet at replacement threshold.
Terminal torque check found two units at 6.2 Nm versus spec 8.0 Nm. Re-torqued and witnessed by QE.
Moisture content in oil measured 42 ppm, action limit 30 ppm. Tank scheduled for full drain and dry-out.
Sensor failed self-test at startup, recovered after power cycle. Logged for trending.
Hi-pot test failure at 2.5 kV, breakdown to ground through slot insulation. Coil tagged for teardown analysis.
Argon flow rate fluctuated between 14 and 16 L/min, setpoint 15. Regulator diaphragm suspected.
Internal short circuit detected during final QA; impedance dropped to 0.08 ohm from expected 4.5 ohm. Unit segregated for destructive analysis.
Pinhole leak identified at elbow joint upstream of pump P-2, drip rate approximately 1 drop per minute. Temporary clamp applied, weld repair scheduled.
